definition: A pension plan maintained under collective bargaining agreements involving multiple unrelated employers, typically in a single industry. Multiple companies contribute to a shared fund that pays retirement benefits to workers. When one employer leaves, the remaining employers bear a greater share of the plan's obligations, which is why withdrawal liability exists.
examples: The IAM National Pension Fund is a multiemployer plan to which multiple employers contributed on behalf of their workers represented by the International Association of Machinists.
